mentions.note: Caller bought at $63, sold at $83; now trading $72–77 range; Cramer likes it for next 1–2 years; reasonable growth and scale, market still has appetite for new supply.
Part of software rotation out; like DoorDash and Reddit, suffering as investors favor hardware only; capital flows negative despite solid business.
Definitely buy; strong growth away from data center; selling at 25x earnings; autonomous vehicle rollout positive; exactly what market wants.
Down 29% from ATH despite 40% EPS growth YoY. Robo-taxi fears (Tesla Waymo) overblown; Uber's 200M users network essential for autonomous scaling. Cheap valuation for first time in years.
Cramer's preferred rideshare; very cheap at current levels; better risk-reward than Lyft despite aggressive Lyft buyback program.
Long-term great value as global ride-share monopolist; in everyone's app, well-known worldwide; regard same way as DoorDash and Airbnb—brand companies from last 6-7 years.
Down 25% from highs; robo-taxi disruption fears overblown; partnerships with Nvidia-powered AVs (Waymo, Zoox, etc.) strengthen ride-share moat; 23x 2026 earnings = cheap.
Caller John bought at high 80s; Cramer insists stay long-term—company taking over the world, short-term volatility ending, stock way too cheap.
